violations.text: The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Cuisinart, and the vegetable chopper) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 10 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer logs are to be maintained daily. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Yellow noodles, prepared in advance, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Chicken was observed held at 124 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Dumplings observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in a pan directly below cooling noodles in the small prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Shell eggs observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit on a shelf below burners in the kitchen. Shell eggs must be held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Eggs were placed in the refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Yellow noodles and vermicelli noodles were placed hot in the prep units in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Peanut sauce was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered container greater than 4 inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Utensils in the cooking area are displayed with the food contact surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026
The floors have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Noodles were placed hot in the prep unit in a covered container greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the basement storage area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025

Peanut sauce was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Noodles were placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Working containers of some ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food, stored in old food containers still with original labels of other foods. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Spoons in dry ingredients observed with the handles in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
Single use items observed being used for food prep or storage. Single use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and front service area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the prep unit, slicer, and kitchen carts have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Numerous stickers observed on equipment. Chest freezers in the basement have an accumulation of frost. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
The dumpster area has an accumulation of leaf litter and debris. Dumpster areas must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 01/01/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen and front service areas. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/28/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2023
The slicer was not in good repair, and must be removed from the establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
The gaskets on the prep unit were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: prep units wok unit underside of shelves New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/06/2023
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2023
The floor beneath the cooking area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2023
